subject: Careers
topic: Technology risks, responsibility, and careers in B.C.

teacher_guide.title: Career 8 Teacher Guide — Technology risks and careers in B.C.
teacher_guide.overview: This multi-page teacher guide supports a multi-day workflow around digital risks, responsibility, and careers in British Columbia.
teacher_guide.essential_question: How can students connect technology risks to responsible decisions and career pathways in B.C.?
teacher_guide.opening_frame: Today we are connecting what students notice in media to what they may need to understand in future workplaces.

timing.activity: Launch the unit and frame the essential question.
Guide students through the follow-along and post-film discussion.
Transition to project tools and research planning.

teacher_notes: Keep the opening frame visible at the start of class.
Return to the timing map when discussion runs long.
teacher_guide.project_prompt: Students will choose one technology risk, connect it to a real B.C. career pathway, and explain a responsible response.

model.title: Teacher model
model.sample: A strong model connects the named risk, one concrete consequence, and one career-linked response.

teacher_guide.assessment_focus: Assessment focus: accurate risk explanation, responsible response, and clear connection to a B.C. career pathway.

task_sheet.title: Student Packet — Technology risks, responsibility, and careers in B.C.
instructions: Before watching, preview the key risk ideas.
While watching, record short follow-along notes.
After watching, use the matching bank and research planner to prepare your project.

tasks.label: Part A
tasks.prompt: Before watching: note one technology risk you already know about.

tasks.label: Part B
tasks.prompt: While watching: record two follow-along notes about privacy, misinformation, or algorithm influence.

tasks.label: Part C
tasks.prompt: Post-film discussion: continue your notes by naming one harm and one responsible response.

tasks.label: Part D
tasks.prompt: Matching bank: match one risk topic to a career pathway in B.C.

tasks.label: Part E
tasks.prompt: Research planner: choose your project topic and record the questions you need to investigate.

embedded_supports: Use the matching bank as a reference tool before you choose a topic.
Keep the research planner in point form first.
success_criteria: I used the packet as a staged workflow.
I used the matching bank as a reference tool.
I completed the final checklist before handing in the project.
